epigraph.text: Better is a little with righteousness than great revenues with injustice.
epigraph.quote: Möbius transformations are the simplest conformal maps that are not affine.

content.text:
Möbius transformations (also called linear fractional transformations) 
form a rich family of conformal maps with elegant algebraic and geometric 
properties.

content.title: Möbius Transformation
content.content:
A **Möbius transformation** is a 
function of the form
\[
 T(z) = \frac{az + b}{cz + d}, \quad \text{where } ad - bc \neq 0.
\]
The condition $ad - bc \neq 0$ ensures $T$ is not constant.

content.text:
We extend $T$ to the Riemann sphere $\hat{\C}$ by defining 
$T(-d/c) = \infty$ and $T(\infty) = a/c$ (or $\infty$ if $c = 0$).

content.title: Properties of Möbius Transformations
content.content:
1. Möbius transformations form a group under composition.

2. Every Möbius transformation is conformal on $\hat{\C}$.

3. Möbius transformations map circles and lines to circles and lines.

4. Given any two sets of three distinct points $\{z_1, z_2, z_3\}$ 
 and $\{w_1, w_2, w_3\}$, there exists a unique Möbius 
 transformation with $T(z_k) = w_k$.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Every Möbius transformation is a composition of translations $z \mapsto z + b$, 
dilations $z \mapsto az$ ($a \neq 0$), and inversions $z \mapsto 1/z$.

Translations and dilations obviously preserve circles and lines. For 
inversion: the equation $|z - a| = r$ (a circle) transforms under 
$w = 1/z$ to another circle or line, as can be verified algebraically.

content.title: Cross-Ratio
content.content:
The **cross-ratio** of four distinct points 
$z_1, z_2, z_3, z_4$ is
\[
 (z_1, z_2; z_3, z_4) = \frac{(z_1 - z_3)(z_2 - z_4)}{(z_1 - z_4)(z_2 - z_3)}.
\]

content.title: Cross-Ratio is Preserved
content.content:
Möbius transformations preserve cross-ratios:
\[
 (T(z_1), T(z_2); T(z_3), T(z_4)) = (z_1, z_2; z_3, z_4).
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.text: This invariant is key to constructing Möbius transformations.

content.title: Upper half-plane to unit disk
content.problem:
The Möbius transformation
\[
 T(z) = \frac{z - i}{z + i}
\]
maps the upper half-plane $\{\im(z) > 0\}$ conformally onto the unit 
disk $\{|w| < 1\}$, with $T(i) = 0$ and $T(\infty) = 1$.

content.title: Disk automorphisms
content.problem:
All conformal automorphisms of the unit disk have the form
\[
 T(z) = e^{i\theta} \frac{z - a}{1 - \bar{a}z}, \quad |a| < 1, \theta \in \R.
\]

marginNotes.text:
When he established the heavens, I was 
there; when he drew a circle on the face of the deep.

marginNotes.author: August Ferdinand Möbius
marginNotes.text:
These transformations preserve 
the fundamental structure of the sphere.
